description_long: This is a two-story Mediterranean-style house with a beige stucco exterior and a prominent terracotta-tiled gable roof. The front facade features a large arched wooden door, flanked by three rectangular windows with wooden shutters, and a small square window near the roofline. The roof includes a single chimney and a small bell tower structure on one end, adding architectural interest. The rear and side walls are mostly plain, with no visible windows or doors, emphasizing the front as the main point of entry. The roof tiles are weathered, giving the house an aged, authentic appearance. The overall structure is solid and rectangular, typical of traditional Mediterranean urban homes.
description: A two-story Mediterranean house with a beige stucco exterior, terracotta-tiled gable roof, and a prominent arched wooden entry door with shuttered windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing the arched wooden door, three shuttered windows, a small square window, and the terracotta-tiled roof with chimney.
description_view_1: Side view displaying a plain beige stucco wall, the gable roof with visible chimney and bell tower, and no windows or doors.
description_view_2: Rear view with a featureless beige wall and the full expanse of the terracotta-tiled roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the bell tower structure on the roof and a plain stucco wall with no openings.
attribute_entryway_design: Arched wooden door with no steps or porch, flanked by shuttered windows and a small square window above.
